# Crosslinking Prompt Template

Use this template when adding a new blog post to identify strategic crosslinking opportunities.

## Content Clusters (as of November 2025)

### ADHD & Productivity Cluster
- **Hub:** 2025-11-17-i-thought-work-was-boring-turns-out-i-have-adhd
- **Posts:** ADHD family life, Solutions Log, Better Than Before (habits)
- **Keywords:** focus, systems, habits, attention, productivity, work patterns

### Fatherhood & Parenting Cluster  
- **Hub:** 2025-09-29-five-things-to-do-every-day-to-be-a-happier-dad
- **Posts:** Digital camera, Phone number safety, Anxious Generation, Going All In (obsessions), Building Something Real
- **Keywords:** presence, parenting, kids, family time, safety, digital wellness

### Personal Systems & Reflection Cluster
- **Hub:** 2023-02-16-how-one-index-card-can-drastically-improve-your-journaling
- **Posts:** Annual themes, Solutions log, 35 things learned, Better Than Before
- **Keywords:** journaling, reflection, themes, goals, personal growth, systems

### Life Philosophy & Growth Cluster
- **Posts:** It costs nothing to be nice, Leaders don't tiptoe, 35 things learned
- **Keywords:** values, leadership, growth, life lessons, character

## Crosslinking Strategy Checklist

### For New Posts:

1. **Identify Primary Cluster**
   - Which existing cluster does this post belong to?
   - Does it create a new cluster or bridge multiple clusters?

2. **Find 2-3 Strategic Links**
   - Look for posts that share keywords/themes
   - Prioritize contextual relevance over keyword matching
   - Link to hub posts when possible (they have the most authority)

3. **Add Contextual Links**
   - Embed 1-2 links naturally in the main content
   - Make the connection explicit: "I wrote about X" or "This connects to Y"
   - Use descriptive link text, not "click here"

4. **Consider Related Articles Section**
   - For hub posts or comprehensive pieces, add structured "Related Articles"
   - Include 2-3 links with brief descriptions
   - Place at end of post, before footer/metadata

5. **Update Existing Posts**
   - Add 1-2 backlinks from relevant existing posts
   - Focus on high-traffic or hub posts for maximum impact
   - Keep links contextual and natural

### Crosslinking Examples:

**Good:** "This same principle of documenting wins applies to personal life. I've found [simple journaling prompts](/articles/journaling) just as powerful."

**Bad:** "You might also like my [other post about productivity](/articles/productivity)."

### SEO Benefits:
- Creates topical authority through content clusters
- Increases time on site and pages per session  
- Helps search engines understand content relationships
- Reduces bounce rate through internal discovery paths

## Implementation Template

When adding crosslinks to existing posts, use this pattern:

```markdown
## Related Articles

- [Post Title](/articles/slug/) - Brief description of why it's relevant
- [Post Title](/articles/slug/) - Brief description of connection
- [Post Title](/articles/slug/) - How it extends this topic
```

Keep descriptions under 10 words and focus on the connection, not just the topic.
